name: Books
description: Famous novels, authors, and literary characters

questions.q: Who wrote the 1813 novel 'Pride and Prejudice'?

source.notes: Austen's second published novel; features Elizabeth Bennet and Mr. Darcy.

questions.q: Who is the author of 'To Kill a Mockingbird'?

source.notes: Published 1960; won the Pulitzer Prize for Fiction in 1961.

questions.q: Who wrote 'The Great Gatsby'?

source.notes: Published in 1925; set on Long Island during the Jazz Age.

questions.q: Who is the teenage narrator of J.D. Salinger's 'The Catcher in the Rye'?

source.notes: Published in 1951; a defining coming-of-age novel.

questions.q: On what street do Harry Potter's Muggle aunt and uncle live at Number 4?

source.notes: The Dursleys' home in fictional Little Whinging, Surrey.

questions.q: In George Orwell's '1984', what chilling phrase appears on posters throughout Oceania?

source.notes: Published 1949; Big Brother is the face of the ruling Party.

questions.q: The HBO series 'Game of Thrones' is based on which novel series by George R.R. Martin?
choices: A Song of Ice and Fire

source.notes: The first book in the series is titled 'A Game of Thrones' (1996).

questions.q: Who wrote 'Jane Eyre', published in 1847 under the pen name Currer Bell?

source.notes: Eldest of the Brontë sisters; also wrote 'Shirley' and 'Villette'.

questions.q: Who wrote 'Wuthering Heights', set on the Yorkshire moors?

source.notes: Her only novel, published 1847 under the pen name Ellis Bell.

questions.q: Who wrote 'The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy'?

source.notes: Started as a 1978 BBC radio series before becoming a novel in 1979.

questions.q: Who is the author of the 2003 novel 'The Da Vinci Code'?

source.notes: Part of the Robert Langdon series, adapted into a 2006 film with Tom Hanks.

questions.q: Who is the recurring Harvard symbologist protagonist of 'The Da Vinci Code' and 'Angels & Demons'?

source.notes: Played by Tom Hanks in the film adaptations.

questions.q: Victorian novelist Mary Ann Evans published under what male pen name?

source.notes: Wrote 'Middlemarch', 'Silas Marner', and 'The Mill on the Floss'.

questions.q: 'It was the best of times, it was the worst of times' is the opening line of which Charles Dickens novel?

source.notes: Set in London and Paris before and during the French Revolution.

questions.q: 'Call me Ishmael' is the famous opening line of which 1851 novel?

source.notes: Herman Melville's novel; Ishmael is the narrator aboard the whaler Pequod.

questions.q: Which obsessive captain hunts the white whale in Herman Melville's 'Moby-Dick'?

source.notes: Ahab commands the whaler Pequod and is driven by revenge.

questions.q: Who wrote 'The Alchemist', the 1988 allegorical novel about a shepherd named Santiago?

source.notes: Brazilian author; 'The Alchemist' has been translated into over 80 languages.

questions.q: Which British author created James Bond, writing 12 novels and two short-story collections?

source.notes: First Bond novel 'Casino Royale' published 1953; Fleming was a former Naval Intelligence officer.

questions.q: Who wrote the acclaimed graphic novel 'Watchmen', featuring Rorschach and Dr Manhattan?

source.notes: Published 1986–87 by DC Comics, illustrated by Dave Gibbons.

questions.q: The film 'Blade Runner' is based on which Philip K. Dick novel?
choices: The Man in the High Castle
Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?

source.notes: Published in 1968; Ridley Scott's film adaptation came out in 1982.

questions.q: Which Swedish author wrote the Millennium trilogy that began with 'The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo'?

source.notes: Larsson died in 2004 before publication; all three books became posthumous bestsellers.

questions.q: John Steinbeck took the title 'Of Mice and Men' from a poem by which Scottish poet?

source.notes: From 'To a Mouse' (1785): 'The best-laid schemes o' mice an' men / Gang aft agley.'
